prophecies.a: 沃尔西主教永远不会到达约克城。
prophecies.a_en: Cardinal Wolsey shall never reach York.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1530年，红衣主教托马斯·沃尔西从伦敦出发前往约克就任大主教，但途中在莱斯特被捕，随后于1530年11月29日病逝，确实从未到达约克。此预言见于最早的1641年出版物。
prophecies.verdict_en: In 1530, Cardinal Thomas Wolsey set out from London to York to be enthroned as Archbishop, but was arrested en route at Leicester and died on November 29, 1530, never reaching York. This prophecy appears in the earliest 1641 publication.

prophecies.q: 关于亨利八世宗教改革
prophecies.a: 修道院之墙将被推倒，牧场上将建起宫殿。
prophecies.a_en: The walls of abbeys shall be pulled down, and palaces built upon their pastures.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1536-1541年，亨利八世发动宗教改革，解散英格兰所有修道院，大量修道院建筑被拆毁，土地被贵族购买用于建造宅邸。此预言可见于早期文献。
prophecies.verdict_en: During 1536-1541, Henry VIII's Dissolution of the Monasteries saw all English monasteries disbanded, their buildings demolished, and lands purchased by nobles for building mansions. This prophecy appears in early sources.

prophecies.a: 外国入侵者的舰队将被风暴击败，德雷克将是英格兰之子中的英雄。
prophecies.a_en: The fleet of foreign invaders shall be beaten by storms, and Drake shall be the hero among the sons of England.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1588年，西班牙无敌舰队进攻英格兰，弗朗西斯·德雷克率英格兰舰队迎击，加之风暴肆虐，无敌舰队大败。然而此预言的具体措辞可能经后人润色，早期版本中并未明确提及德雷克。
prophecies.verdict_en: In 1588, the Spanish Armada attacked England. Francis Drake led the English fleet against them, and combined with devastating storms, the Armada was defeated. However, the specific wording mentioning Drake may have been embellished by later editors; early versions do not explicitly name him.

prophecies.a: 伦敦的骄傲将在火焰中化为灰烬，特里尼蒂教堂的塔楼将被烧毁。
prophecies.q_en: On the Great Fire of London
prophecies.a_en: London's pride shall be brought to ashes, and the tower of Trinity Church shall be burned.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1666年9月2-6日，伦敦大火烧毁了城市中心大部分区域，包括13200栋房屋、87座教堂和圣保罗大教堂。此预言是希普顿修女最广为人知的应验预言之一，见于1641年首版出版物（早于大火25年）。
prophecies.verdict_en: September 2-6, 1666, the Great Fire of London destroyed most of the city center, including 13,200 houses, 87 churches, and St. Paul's Cathedral. This is one of Mother Shipton's most widely cited fulfilled prophecies, appearing in the 1641 first edition (25 years before the fire).

prophecies.q: 关于铁船（疑为辛德利伪作）
prophecies.a: 铁制的船将在水上漂浮，如同木板和羊毛一般轻松。
prophecies.a_en: Iron in the water shall float, as easy as a wooden boat.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1843年，伊桑巴德·金德姆·布鲁内尔建造的「大不列颠号」成为首艘横渡大西洋的铁壳蒸汽船。但此预言极可能是1862年辛德利伪造，因为铁船在1862年已是常见事物。
prophecies.verdict_en: In 1843, Isambard Kingdom Brunel's SS Great Britain became the first iron-hulled steamship to cross the Atlantic. However, this prophecy is very likely an 1862 Hindley forgery, as iron ships were already commonplace by then.

prophecies.q: 关于无马马车（疑为辛德利伪作）
prophecies.a: 马车无马亦可行，灾祸遍布世间生。
prophecies.a_en: Carriages without horses shall go, and accidents fill the world with woe.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1886年，卡尔·本茨制造了第一辆汽油驱动汽车。汽车的普及确实带来了大量交通事故。但此预言几乎可以确定是1862年辛德利伪造，因其精确描述了19世纪中叶已可预见的技术发展。
prophecies.verdict_en: In 1886, Karl Benz built the first gasoline-powered automobile. Cars did bring widespread traffic accidents. However, this prophecy is almost certainly an 1862 Hindley forgery, as it precisely describes technological developments foreseeable by the mid-19th century.

prophecies.q: 关于环球通信（疑为辛德利伪作）
prophecies.a: 思绪环球瞬息间，万事转瞬如云烟。
prophecies.a_en: Around the world thoughts shall fly, in the twinkling of an eye.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1866年，跨大西洋海底电报电缆成功铺设，实现了洲际即时通信。此预言同样极可能为辛德利伪作——1862年时电报技术已成熟，跨洋电缆正在铺设中。
prophecies.verdict_en: In 1866, the transatlantic telegraph cable was successfully laid, enabling intercontinental instant communication. This prophecy is likewise very likely a Hindley forgery — by 1862, telegraph technology was mature and transatlantic cables were being laid.

prophecies.q: 关于海底旅行（疑为辛德利伪作）
prophecies.a: 人们将在海面之下行走、骑行、安睡、交谈。
prophecies.a_en: Under water men shall walk, shall ride, shall sleep, shall talk.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 潜水技术和潜艇在19世纪下半叶快速发展。1870年代出现了实用潜水装备，20世纪潜艇成为重要军事装备。但此预言同样属于辛德利伪作嫌疑范畴，儒勒·凡尔纳1870年出版《海底两万里》反映了当时社会对海底探索的普遍想象。
prophecies.verdict_en: Diving technology and submarines developed rapidly in the latter half of the 19th century. Practical diving equipment appeared in the 1870s, and submarines became major military assets in the 20th century. This prophecy also falls under Hindley forgery suspicion — Jules Verne's Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea (1870) reflects the era's widespread imagination about undersea exploration.

prophecies.q: 关于飞行（疑为辛德利伪作）
prophecies.a: 人们将在空中飞翔，如同鸟儿一般。
prophecies.a_en: In the air men shall be seen, in white, in black, and in green.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1903年12月17日，莱特兄弟在基蒂霍克完成人类首次动力飞行。此预言同属辛德利伪作嫌疑——19世纪中叶热气球飞行已普及，动力飞行的设想已广泛存在。
prophecies.verdict_en: On December 17, 1903, the Wright brothers achieved the first powered flight at Kitty Hawk. This prophecy is also suspected Hindley forgery — balloon flight was common by the mid-19th century, and the concept of powered flight was widely discussed.

prophecies.q: 关于黄金从溪流中被发现
prophecies.a: 黄金将在溪流和石头中被发现，在一块尚未被知晓的土地上。
prophecies.a_en: Gold shall be found in a land not yet known, midst stone and stream.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1848年1月，詹姆斯·马歇尔在加利福尼亚萨特磨坊的河流中发现黄金，引发加州淘金热。然而加利福尼亚在16世纪尚未被欧洲人广泛了解，符合「尚未被知晓的土地」的描述。此预言来源存疑，可能经后人添加或改编。
prophecies.verdict_en: In January 1848, James Marshall discovered gold in the American River at Sutter's Mill, California, triggering the Gold Rush. California was not widely known to Europeans in the 16th century, fitting 'a land not yet known.' However, the provenance of this prophecy is questionable and may have been added or adapted later.

prophecies.a: 两次伟大的战争将降临，两场血腥的屠杀将使世界颤抖。
prophecies.a_en: Two great wars shall come, two bloody slaughters that shall make the world tremble.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 第一次世界大战（1914-1918）和第二次世界大战（1939-1945）确实是人类历史上最惨烈的两次全球冲突。但此预言的原始出处不明，极可能是20世纪的后人追溯归于希普顿修女，而非真正的早期预言。
prophecies.verdict_en: World War I (1914-1918) and World War II (1939-1945) were indeed the two deadliest global conflicts in human history. However, the original source of this prophecy is unclear and is very likely a 20th-century retrospective attribution to Mother Shipton rather than a genuine early prediction.

prophecies.q: 关于女性的变化（疑为辛德利伪作）
prophecies.a: 女人将穿得像男人一样，剪短头发，骑在马鞍上。
prophecies.q_en: On changes in women (suspected Hindley forgery)
prophecies.a_en: Women shall dress like men and trousers wear, and cut off all their locks of hair.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 20世纪初，女性解放运动使女性开始穿着长裤、剪短发。1920年代的「飞来波女郎」（Flappers）风潮是标志性事件。但此预言属于辛德利伪作嫌疑——19世纪中叶女权运动已兴起，女性着装变革的趋势已可预见。
prophecies.verdict_en: In the early 20th century, the women's liberation movement led women to wear trousers and cut their hair short. The 1920s Flapper movement was emblematic. However, this prophecy is suspected Hindley forgery — the women's rights movement was already underway by the mid-19th century, making dress reform foreseeable.

prophecies.q: 关于世界末日（已证实为辛德利伪作）
prophecies.a: 世界将走到终点，在一八八一年。
prophecies.q_en: On the end of the world (confirmed Hindley forgery)
prophecies.a_en: The world to an end shall come, in eighteen hundred and eighty-one.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1881年世界并未终结。1862年，出版商查尔斯·辛德利在其出版的《希普顿修女预言集》中加入此诗句。辛德利后来公开承认这是自己的伪作，成为希普顿预言伪造争议的标志性事件。
prophecies.verdict_en: The world did not end in 1881. In 1862, publisher Charles Hindley inserted this verse into his edition of Mother Shipton's prophecies. Hindley later publicly admitted it was his own fabrication, making this the most iconic case in the Mother Shipton forgery controversy.

prophecies.q: 关于德雷克与西班牙人
prophecies.a: 德雷克将扬帆起航，成为英格兰的庇护者。他将给敌人带来恐惧，使西班牙人的骄傲化为乌有。
prophecies.a_en: Drake shall sail and be England's shield. He shall strike fear in the foe, and bring the pride of Spain to naught.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 弗朗西斯·德雷克（1540-1596）确实成为都铎时代最著名的海军英雄。1588年他参与击败西班牙无敌舰队，此前还完成了环球航行（1577-1580）并多次劫掠西班牙殖民地。但此预言的确切出处存疑，可能经后人追溯编撰。
prophecies.verdict_en: Francis Drake (1540-1596) indeed became the most famous naval hero of the Tudor era. In 1588, he helped defeat the Spanish Armada, having previously circumnavigated the globe (1577-1580) and raided Spanish colonies. However, the exact provenance of this prophecy is uncertain and may have been retrospectively compiled.
